---
title: "Your Next Application"
---

## Beyond the basics

Your first app had one input and one output.

Real dashboards have:

- Layout structure (sidebars, columns, tabs)
    - `shiny.express.ui.*()`
- Multiple coordinated outputs
- Summary statistics (value boxes)
    - `ui.value_box()`
- Shared reactive computations
    - `@reactive.calc`
- Interactive plots (jupyter widgets)
    - `shinywidgets.render_*()`

## Example application

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| components: [viewer]
#| layout: vertical
#| viewerHeight: 600

{{< include /../code/demos/app-05-tips.py >}}

## file: requirements.txt
ridgeplot
```

## Layout components

```python
{{< include /../code/demos/app-05a-tips-layout.py >}}
```



## Value boxes

```python
from shiny.express import render, ui
from faicons import icon_svg

with ui.value_box(showcase=icon_svg("dollar-sign")):
    "Average bill"

    @render.text
    def avg_bill():
        return f"${filtered()['total_bill'].mean():.2f}"
```

## `@reactive.calc` for shared filtering

```python
from shiny import reactive

@reactive.calc
def filtered():
    df = tips.copy()
    df = df[df["total_bill"] <= input.bill()]
    df = df[df["time"].isin(input.time())]
    return df
```

All outputs call `filtered()` — the filter runs once per change.

## Full app structure

```python
ui.page_opts(title="Tips Dashboard", fillable=True)

with ui.sidebar():
    # inputs ...

@reactive.calc
def filtered():
    # shared data transform ...

with ui.layout_columns():
    with ui.value_box(...):
        @render.text
        def avg_bill(): ...

    with ui.card():
        @render.plot
        def scatter(): ...

    with ui.card():
        @render.data_frame
        def table(): ...
```

## Exercise
